title: Luhn-Algorithm BIN Expansion with Sequential Card6 Iteration
narrative: Fraudster generates mathematically valid card numbers using Luhn checksum algorithm, targeting a single BIN prefix while incrementing the card6 field systematically. A merchant processor observes 47 declined transactions over 22 minutes, all sharing identical BIN (first 6 digits) but varying in positions 7-12, with card6 values incrementing sequentially (1, 2, 3, 4, etc.). All attempts originate from single IP (dist1=0 across all), same P_emaildomain (tempmail-style), and identical addr1. TransactionAmt locked at $1.00 for validation testing. Card1_txn_count_1day shows 0 for all attempts, confirming never-seen cards. Approval rate 0% with consistent auth decline code 05 (do not honor). Time between attempts ranges 18-35 seconds, suggesting automated script with modest throttling. ProductCD remains 'W' (digital wallet funding) across sequence.
indicators: 40+ transactions sharing identical first 6 digits (BIN) with sequential variation in positions 7-15
Card6 field increments linearly (1, 2, 3...) across transaction sequence within 20-30 minute window
100% decline rate with uniform auth response code (typically 05, 14, or 51)
Dist1=0 and identical P_emaildomain across all attempts indicating single device/session
TransactionAmt fixed at $1.00-$5.00 for all validation attempts
Card1_txn_count_1day=0 for every card number tried
Inter-transaction timing 15-45 seconds suggesting scripted automation with rate limiting
summary: A merchant sees dozens of declined $1 transactions in under 30 minutes, all using the same bank identification number (first 6 digits) but with card6 values that increment by one each time (1, 2, 3, 4...), all from the same IP address and disposable email domain.
reasoning: The attacker is generating fake but mathematically valid card numbers using the Luhn algorithm to find active accounts within a specific bank's card range, testing each generated number with small-dollar purchases to avoid detection while hunting for valid credentials to exploit later.
indicator_explanations: Attacker is systematically testing card numbers from the same issuing bank
Sequential counter reveals automated generation of card variations in order
Every single attempt is rejected with the same decline reason code
All attempts originate from one device and email address in same session
Low fixed dollar amounts used to minimize loss during card validation sweep
None of these generated card numbers have any legitimate transaction history
Consistent timing gaps between attempts indicate bot with deliberate throttling
